James.text: According to James 1:19, what should be the first response of a person?
options: B) Be quick to listen, slow to speak, slow to anger

James.text: James 2:14 emphasizes that faith without ___ is dead.
James compares the tongue to a ___ that sets the course of life on fire.
Which chapter in James warns about favoritism toward the rich over the poor?
James 4:7 teaches submission to ___ and resistance to ___.
James 1:27 defines pure religion as caring for orphans and widows and keeping oneself ___ from the world.
Which metaphor does James use for the danger of favoritism?
options: C) A spring that yields bitter water

James.text: What example of patience does James 5:7–8 highlight?
James 3 warns about the tongue, comparing it to all except:
options: A) A small spark setting a forest ablaze
B) A rudder controlling a ship
C) A horse bit controlling the whole animal

James.text: James 5:16 teaches believers to confess sins to:

Joshua.text: Who succeeded Moses as the leader of Israel?
The walls of which city fell after the Israelites marched around it for seven days?
Who were the two spies that Joshua sent into Canaan secretly?
Joshua 1:9 tells Israel to be strong and courageous because:
options: B) God will be with them wherever they go

Joshua.text: How did the Israelites divide the Jordan River when entering Canaan?
options: B) The river dried up temporarily
C) They crossed at a bridge
D) They waited until flood season

Joshua.text: Who tricked Joshua into making a treaty with them by pretending to come from a distant land?

options: A) “As for me and my house, we will serve the Lord.”
B) “Choose this day whom you will serve.”

Joshua.text: Which two leaders’ bones were carried into Canaan?
How did the Israelites defeat Ai after their first failed attack?
options: A) Ambush from behind the city

Joshua.text: What was the condition God set for Israel to prosper in Joshua 1?
options: B) Reading the Book of the Law continually and obeying it

1 Kings.text: Which prophet confronted King Ahab about Naboth’s vineyard?
What request did Solomon make from God at Gibeon?
Which king built the Temple in Jerusalem?
Who challenged 450 prophets of Baal on Mount Carmel?
Which queen visited Solomon to test his wisdom?
1 Kings 11 mentions Solomon’s downfall was due to:
Who succeeded Solomon as king of Israel?
Which king healed Naaman the Syrian?
Which prophet predicted the division of the kingdom after Solomon’s reign?

Proverbs.text: According to Proverbs 3:5–6, trust in the Lord with all your heart and:
options: A) Lean on your own understanding
C) Acknowledge Him in all your ways

Proverbs.text: Proverbs 6 warns that laziness leads to:

Romans.text: Romans 3:23 says all have sinned and:
options: B) Fall short of the glory of God

General Bible Knowledge.text: How many books are in the Bible?
Who wrote most of the Psalms?
